text:
Tomorrow will be hard to wait for, I know. That particular kind of dread — the night before — has its own weight, and I won't pretend otherwise.

But here is what I do know. The people taking care of you tomorrow have done this many times. They trained for exactly this. Your job is simply to show up, and you have already done harder things than that.

Fear before a procedure is not weakness. It is just your brain doing what brains do — running scenarios, staying alert. You can let it run. It does not have to mean anything is wrong.

Tonight, try to be somewhere warm. Eat something simple. Let yourself be distracted by something small and forgettable. You do not need to feel brave right now.

And when tomorrow comes, it will move fast. You will be through it before the slow part of waiting is even a memory. I will be thinking of you the whole time.

You are going to be okay.
